messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following co-working space membership privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"WorkHub collects your name, company name, contact details, and payment information to manage your membership. Access logs are collected when you use your keycard to enter the building. We use Stripe for payment processing. Data is retained for the duration of your membership plus 2 years. You may access or request deletion of your data by contacting us at privacy@workhub.com."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) implied for membership management. Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) implied for access logging. Neither is explicitly stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Not applicable. Data collected does not constitute special category data.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Membership management is stated. Access log purpose (security vs. billing vs. occupancy monitoring) is not specified.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is proportionate to membership administration and building access.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Membership data: duration plus 2 years. Access logs retention is not separately stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access and erasure referenced. Other rights absent.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Stripe named as payment processor but no DPA reference provided. Access control system provider not disclosed.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] Not addressed. Stripe processes data in the US — no safeguard mechanism stated.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis not stated), Art. 5 (access log purpose and retention not specified), Art. 15-21 (most rights absent), Art. 28 (Stripe DPA not referenced, access control provider not disclosed), Art. 46 (Stripe US transfer safeguards absent), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State lawful basis for each processing activity including access logging
- Specify the purpose of access log collection and add a separate retention period
- Add full data subject rights section
- Reference Stripe DPA and disclose access control system provider
- Address Stripe US transfer safeguards (SCCs)
- Confirm DPO requirement and disclose if applicable
- Add breach notification commitment
